classification.reasoning: The patch introduces new logic to handle out-of-memory errors by broadcasting kill requests to other nodes when failures occur during distributed result streaming. It adds new imports, fields, constructor parameters, and methods, but the overall structure of the classes remains unchanged. The changes are mostly additive and involve some control flow adjustments and new method calls. The localization evidence shows a mix of 'git_exact' and 'git_pickaxe' findings with high confidence, indicating some fuzzy matching due to added code but no deep structural refactoring or redesign. Therefore, the patch complexity fits best as TYPE_II, involving whitespace/formatting drift and moderate code additions requiring fuzzy matching.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added a static import for the broadcastKill method from TransportDistributedResultAction.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable direct usage of broadcastKill without qualifying it, facilitating job kill broadcasts.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Imported KillJobsNodeRequest and KillResponse classes related to job kill actions.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To support handling and sending kill requests and responses for jobs within the distributing consumer.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added two new fields: killNodeAction (an ActionExecutor for kill requests) and localNodeId (string).
hunk_descriptions.intent: To hold references needed to send kill requests and identify the local node for job termination signaling.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Modified constructor signature to accept killNodeAction and localNodeId parameters.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable the consumer to send kill requests and know the local node identity during instantiation.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Assigned the new killNodeAction and localNodeId fields in the constructor body.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To initialize the new fields for use in job kill signaling and local node identification.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Replaced logic on failure requests to broadcast a kill signal to downstream nodes, close iterator, and complete future exceptionally instead of just continuing or closing quietly.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: DistributingConsumer, method handling failure requests (likely in response processing)
hunk_descriptions.intent: To ensure that when a failure is detected, all downstream nodes are informed to kill the job, preventing hanging or inconsistent states.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added imports for KillJobsNodeAction, KillJobsNodeRequest, and KillResponse related to job kill functionality.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To support creation and execution of job kill actions within the consumer factory.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added a new field killNodeAction of type ActionExecutor for handling kill job requests.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To enable the factory to provide kill action executors to consumers it creates.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Initialized killNodeAction in the constructor to execute KillJobsNodeAction on the node's client.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To set up the kill action executor for use when creating distributing consumers.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Updated the create method call to pass killNodeAction and local node ID as additional parameters to the DistributingConsumer constructor.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To provide the necessary kill action executor and local node identification to the consumer instances it creates.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Added import of UUID class.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To support usage of UUID type in this class, likely for job identification.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Changed a boolean parameter in the superclass constructor call from false to true to enable request handling on transport layer.
hunk_descriptions.intent: To ensure that all requests are handled to prevent jobs from getting stuck due to unhandled transport requests.

hunk_descriptions.what_changed: Replaced inline kill request logic on missing RootTask with a call to the new broadcastKill method; extracted broadcastKill as a static method to send kill requests to all nodes except the sender; added detailed reason message for kill requests.
hunk_descriptions.where_in_code: TransportDistributedResultAction, method handling received results without RootTask
hunk_descriptions.intent: To centralize and standardize the logic for broadcasting kill requests when job context is missing, preventing handler merges from hanging.
